👻 Phantom stops & the recovered model field

PR for #3035 + #3034. Two defects in agent-usage.jsonl: ~38% of rows are events that correspond to no agent execution anywhere, and four fields were constant on 100% of 11,319 rows. Every number below is measured, not estimated.

Why all three signals are required

Toggle the signals. Any single signal alone misclassifies a real agent — that is the whole reason the gate is a conjunction.

The conjunction is exact on real data

859 live ledger rows. Note the 27 attributed rows with duration 0 — they are not gated, because they have a type. That is precisely why duration alone would be the wrong signal.

signalunknown rows (477)attributed rows (382)
transcript exists0382 / 382
duration_ms == 0477 / 47727 / 382  ← not gated
agent_id in ~/.claude/projects01 each
unique agent ids20073583 · overlap 0

Corroborated independently: of 819 transcript_path values across the ledgers, only 340 exist on disk.

#3034 — model recovered from the transcript

The field read input.tool_input?.model, but CC sends no tool_input at SubagentStop — so it was the literal string "unknown" on every row. The transcript has it, and analyzeTranscript() was already reading that file.

modeltranscripts
claude-opus-4-8208
claude-fable-562
claude-sonnet-541
claude-haiku-4-524

335 / 340 = 98.5% recovery on the population the hook actually sees. Unrecoverable cases write null, never the string "unknown" — null is a fact, a constant string is a lie.

DELETED, not fixed:
  agent_name   read tool_input.name        → CC never sends it   → was null   x11,319
  output_len   read agent_output / output  → CC never sends them → was 0      x11,319

A key that is 100% constant reads as data. Absence is honest.

The bug this change surfaced

Three unrelated tests failed. The cause was not the new code.

try {
    trackEvent(...)        ← user profiling.  If this THROWS…
    appendAnalytics(...)   ← …the analytics row is never written.
} catch { /* silent */ }

The record you most want when something is going wrong was the one
destroyed by something going wrong. Profiling now has its own guard,
pinned by a regression test.
